front.title: SR-TE Path Based on Quality-Aware Dynamic Adaptive Adjustment

abstract.t: This document defines a framework for quality-aware dynamic adaptive adjustment of Segment Lists within an SR-TE Candidate Path. It addresses the limitation of static weight assignment, which cannot adapt to dynamic changes in path quality (e.g., latency, loss, utilization). The framework enables continuous monitoring of end-to- end metrics for each Segment List, computation of updated weights proportional to their relative quality, and application at the policy head-end. This allows the Candidate Path to autonomously optimize traffic distribution towards higher-quality paths, enhancing performance without altering the SR-Policy architecture.
